title: can't talk to a V1 docker registry
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug
Can't pull images from `hub.docker.com`:
```
podman run -i -t openjdk:8-jre-alpine /bin/sh
Trying to pull hub.docker.com/openjdk:8-jre-alpine...ERRO[0000] Error pulling image ref //hub.docker.com/openjdk:8-jre-alpine: Error initializing source docker://hub.docker.com/openjdk:8-jre-alpine: can't talk to a V1 docker registry 
Failed
Error: unable to pull openjdk:8-jre-alpine: 1 error occurred:
	* Error initializing source docker://hub.docker.com/openjdk:8-jre-alpine: can't talk to a V1 docker registry
```

1. `podman run -i -t openjdk:8-jre-alpine /bin/sh`
```
**Describe the results you received:**
Trying to pull hub.docker.com/openjdk:8-jre-alpine...ERRO[0000] Error pulling image ref //hub.docker.com/openjdk:8-jre-alpine: Error initializing source docker://hub.docker.com/openjdk:8-jre-alpine: can't talk to a V1 docker registry 
Failed
Error: unable to pull openjdk:8-jre-alpine: 1 error occurred:
	* Error initializing source docker://hub.docker.com/openjdk:8-jre-alpine: can't talk to a V1 docker registry
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
I expected it to pull the image and run it.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version
Version:            1.4.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.4
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
podman info --debug
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.10.4
  podman version: 1.4.0
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.8.3
  Conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/bin/conmon'
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 0.3.0, commit: unknown'
  Distribution:
    distribution: ubuntu
    version: "16.04"
  MemFree: 6353059840
  MemTotal: 16697331712
  OCIRuntime:
    package: 'cri-o-runc: /usr/lib/cri-o-runc/sbin/runc'
    path: /usr/lib/cri-o-runc/sbin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 17060327424
  SwapTotal: 17060327424
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 8
  hostname: hpspectre
  kernel: 4.15.0-52-generic
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  uptime: 2h 42m 23.2s (Approximately 0.08 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure:
  - default-route-openshift-image-registry.apps.lightbend412.coreostrain.me
  - image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000
  search:
  - hub.docker.com
store:
  ConfigFile: /home/michael/.config/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: vfs
  GraphOptions: null
  GraphRoot: /home/michael/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus: {}
  ImageStore:
    number: 0
  RunRoot: /tmp/1000
  VolumePath: /home/michael/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
VirtualBox is installed on my machine

comments.body:
I believe 1.4.0 has the sysregistriesv2 work...
@mtrmac PTAL - I suspect this is in c/image

Isn’t this just a simple configuration error?

@michael-read Did you specifically intend to make the search path use `hub.docker.com`, or just the common Docker Hub, which uses the `docker.io` hostname?

Notably `docker://docker.io/openjdk:8-jre-alpine` does exist and can be reached.

---

As for the V1 protocol:

c/image has never supported the v1 protocol (which is so dead that the server repository has been removed, and I can’t even quickly find a specification); Docker has removed it from recent versions of its product as well.

It’s _extremely_ unlikely that we would be working on implementing that protocol at this stage.

At best, I can imagine someone contributing a new transport (`docker-v1`?) that could be maintained without interfering with the v2 client.

But, really, why bother? Per https://engineering.docker.com/2019/03/registry-v1-api-deprecation/ even the Docker hub has supposedly stopped serving content using that protocol already.

Yes, so there was no default set, so I just added the following to the config:
[registries.search]
registries = ['hub.docker.com']

Should it be something else like docker.io? Maybe I'll give that a try.

okay, I changed it to docker.io. Maybe that should be configured as the default during installation.

Now I have another error:
```
Trying to pull docker.io/library/openjdk:8-jre-alpine...Getting image source signatures
Copying blob e7c96db7181b done
Copying blob b6abafe80f63 done
Copying blob f910a506b6cb done
Copying config f7a292bbb7 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
ERRO[0010] could not find slirp4netns, the network namespace won't be configured: exec: "slirp4netns": executable file not found in $PATH 
```
I guess I need to find `slirp4netns`, and get it installed. Would have thought the default install would have set up any required dependencies.

What are you installing on?  All of this is setup correctly if you install on Fedora, Centos, RHEL, and I believe it should work correctly in Ubuntu, SUSE.

If you are building and installing from hand, then you need to handle the required packages.
Did you get the packages from the PPC?

I just used:
```
sudo apt install slirp4netns
```
However now, when trying:
```
podman run -i -t openjdk:8-jre-alpine /bin/sh
```
I just get a prompt, without an error:
```
/ # 
```
It appears Podman hasn't been tested on Ubuntu 16.04 LTS.
Are you in a container when the prompt completes?
okay, my apologies. It does appear that I'm in the container. Closing. Thanks for the help!

Got this same error running the below on RHEL8 rootless:
podman search registry.access.redhat.com/rhel8

error:
ERRO[0022] error searching registry "registry.access.redhat.com": couldn't search registry "registry.access.redhat.com": can't talk to a V1 docker registry

This was resolved by adding docker.io to the [registries.search].  For rootless, this is in the config file,
$HOME/.config/containers/registries.conf : 
[registries.search]
registries = ['registry.access.redhat.com', 'registry.redhat.io', 'docker.io']

Simple (but not obvious) fix.

Older/non-patched distribution:
NAME="Red Hat Enterprise Linux"
VERSION="8.2 (Ootpa)"
ID="rhel"
ID_LIKE="fedora"
VERSION_ID="8.2"
PLATFORM_ID="platform:el8"
PRETTY_NAME="Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.2 (Ootpa)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;31"
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:redhat:enterprise_linux:8.2:GA"
HOME_URL="https://www.redhat.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugzilla.redhat.com/"

REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=8.2
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Red Hat Enterprise Linux"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION="8.2"

Also had to add this to the [registries.search] block:
unqualified-search-registries = [""registry.access.redhat.com", docker.io"]

In order to get this command working:
skopeo inspect docker://registry.redhat.io/rhel8/pcp

It was throwing this error without it:
FATA[0024] Error parsing image name "docker://registry.redhat.io/rhel8/pcp": can't talk to a V1 docker registry

Finally, my network is very slow, timing out, running this in a VM on a Mac over wifi.  Even with these changes, I sometimes get the above error but running the command right afterwards succeeds.
What distribution are you using that does not have `docker.io` in there by default?

- As a general practice, please file new issues instead of piling onto closed ones, especially if the details don’t _exactly_ match.
- It’s _extremely_ unlikely that the search path has anything to do with the `registry.redhat.io` failure; `skopeo` _never_ uses the search path for any purpose, to my knowledge. My guess is that the registry in question had a partial outage, where the V2 server was unavailable but the apparently-existing V1 stub continued to be served.
